content.problemStatement:
There are coins of 222 different denominations in Crazyland, 111-cent coins and 222-cent coins. They have two faces - heads and tails.

Your task is to find out the the number of ways to create a linear arrangement of these coins so that their sum is NNN cents. The only condition on the linear arrangement is that the first coin in the arrangement should always have heads up. All other coins could have either tails or heads up.

Take N=2N = 2N=2 as an example. The possible arrangements are (1H,1H)(1H, 1H)(1H,1H), (2H)(2H)(2H), (1H,1T)(1H, 1T)(1H,1T), where HHH is heads and TTT is tails. Therefore there are 333 possible arrangements that sum up to 222-cents.

Note: While counting make sure to differentiate between heads and tails.
content.inputFormat:
First line contains a single integer - TTT, the number of test cases.




TTT lines follow, each containing a single integer NNN, the required sum.
content.outputFormat: For each case the output should be a single integer representing the number of such arrangements possible. As the answer can be very large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
